LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED IN SUBSTANTIAL PART. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION, THOUGH LABELED A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION MOTION, IS, IN SUBSTANCE, A DISCOVERY MOTION AND DOES NOT REQUIRE ANALYSIS OF PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION FACTORS OR THE POSTING OF A BOND. THE BANKRUPTCY COURT'S RECENT DECISION MAKES CLEAR THAT THIS COURT IS EMPOWERED TO RULE ON THIS MOTION AND THAT THERE IS NO BANKRUPTCY RULE THAT AUTHORIZED THE BANKRUPTCY TRUSTEE TO GIVE ACCESS TO THE RECORDS AT ISSUE IN THIS MOTION TO MR. DANKO AND THE O&C CREDITORS GROUP (THE DANKO PARTIES). SEE WORD MINUTES FOR DETAILS. IT DOES NOT AFFECT ANY DOCUMENTS UNRELATED TO THE STEPHENS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: IRENE TOFFT, CSR # 12313

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES IS GRANTED IN PART. FIREMAN'S FUND IS AWARDED $55,884.17 IN FEES AND COSTS AGAINST CROSS-COMPLAINANT O&C CREDITORS GROUP, INC. FIREMAN'S FUND IS ENTITLED TO AN AWARD OF REASONABLE FEES AND COSTS AS A RESULT OF PREVAILING ON ITS ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. WHILE THE COURT DOES NOT QUESTION THAT COUNSEL FOR FIREMAN'S FUND SPENT THE HOURS THAT HE SAYS HE SPENT AND FIREMAN' FUND IS PERMITTED TO HAVE HIM SPEND THOSE HOURS, THE HOURS SPENT EXCEED WHAT WAS REASONABLY NECESSARY FOR THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION, THIS MOTION, O&C'S EX PARTE APPLICATIONS, AND O&C'S DISCOVERY MOTION. BASED ON THE COURT'S INVOLVEMENT IN THOSE MATTERS AND REVIEW OF THE COURT FILE, THE COURT CONCLUDES THAT THE OUTER LIMIT OF REASONABLE HOURS FOR HIGHLY SKILLED COUNSEL FOR ALL OF THESE MATTERS IS 90 HOURS (50 FOR THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION, 20 FOR THIS MOTION, AND 20 FOR THE EX PARTE APPLICATIONS AND DISCOVERY MOTION). WITHOUT EXPRESSING OR IMPLYING ANY CRITICISM OF THE SKILL LEVEL OF COUNSEL FOR FIREMAN'S FUND, AND IN FACT EXPRESSLY FINDING THAT HIS WRITTEN AND ORAL ADVOCACY IS OF A VERY HIGH QUALITY, THE COURT BELIEVES HIS HOURLY RATE OF $740 EXCEEDS A REASONABLE HOURLY RATE FOR THE WORK THAT WAS PERFORMED. IN A LAW FIRM SETTING SUCH AS COUNSEL FOR FIREMAN'S FUND, MUCH OF THE WORK THAT WAS PERFORMED COULD HAVE BEEN DONE BY A JUNIOR OR MID-LEVEL ASSOCIATE AT A LOWER BILLING RATE SUPERVISED BY A SENIOR COUNSEL OR PARTNER AT THE SAME OR HIGHER RATE THAN SOUGHT FOR THIS MOTION. THERE WAS NO REASONABLE NEED FOR ALL THE WORK TO BE DONE BY AN ATTORNEY BILLING $740 PER HOUR. A REASONABLE "BLENDED" RATE FOR THE WORK IS $600 PER HOUR. FIREMAN'S FUND HAS SHOWN THAT ALL $1,884.17 IN COSTS IT IS REQUESTING WERE REASONABLY INCURRED. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Aug-09-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Robert Humphreys of the law offices of Akin Gump (310-229-1000) appears as counsel for cross-defendant/moving party, Fireman's Fund

Attorney, Claire Y. Choo of the law offices of Danko Meredith Trial Lawyers (650-453-3600) appears as counsel for cross-complainant/opposing party, D&C Creditors

No other appearance is made in this matter

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

Court having reviewed the pleading and having heard oral argument, tentative ruling is confirmed with modification as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANT FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY's MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES is GRANTED in part. Fireman's Fund is awarded $55,884.17 in fees and costs against cross-complainant O&C Creditors Group, Inc. Fireman's Fund is entitled to an award of reasonable fees and costs as a result of prevailing on its anti-SLAPP motion. While the court does not question that counsel for Fireman's Fund spent the hours that he says he spent and Fireman' Fund is permitted to have him spend those hours, the hours spent exceed what was reasonably necessary for the anti-SLAPP motion, this motion, O&C's ex parte applications, and O&C's discovery motion. Based on the court's involvement in those matters and review of the court file, the court concludes that the outer limit of reasonable hours for highly skilled counsel for all of these matters is 90 hours (50 for the anti-SLAPP motion, 20 for this motion, and 20 for the ex parte applications and discovery motion). Without expressing or implying any criticism of the skill level of counsel for Fireman's Fund, and in fact expressly finding that his written and oral advocacy is of a very high quality, the court believes his hourly rate of $740 exceeds a reasonable hourly rate for the work that was performed. In a law firm setting such as counsel for Fireman's Fund, much of the work that was performed could have been done by a junior or mid-level associate at a lower billing rate supervised by a senior counsel or partner at the same or higher rate than sought for this motion. There was no reasonable need for all the work to be done by an attorney billing $740 per hour. A reasonable "blended" rate for the work is $600 per hour. Fireman's Fund has shown that all $1,884.17 in costs it is requesting were reasonably incurred. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR No. 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Danko Meredith
Off Calendar Jul-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kyle Withers, Esq. (415-981-0550) of Lubin Olson for Plaintiff.
Claire Choo Esq. (650-453-3600) of Danko Meredith Trial Lawyers for Defendant O & C Creditors and Michael Danko.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares his concerns with the parties, explains privelege log, and unusual circumstances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS and CROSS DEFENDANTS' STEPHENS & STEPHENS XII LLC, STEPHENS & COMPANY LLC, DON STEPHENS and LANE STEPHENS' NICHOLAS KAPRANOS AND NINA SHAPIRSHTEYN'S Motion To Disqualify Danko Meredith is DENIED. Judge Kahn discloses that he has known declarant Richard Zitrin for over thirty years and that Mr. Zitrin is the father of a close friend of Judge Kahn's nephew. Judge Kahn further discloses that the fact that Mr. Zitrin has been retained by the Stephens entities does not cause him to favor or disfavor any of the parties. The parties, citing the recent McDermott decision, appear to agree that the standard governing this motion is whether there is a reasonable probability that the Danko Meredith firm has obtained information that it was not entitled to obtain that it would be used adversely to the Stephens entities. The Stephens entities have not met this standard. The Stephens entities have not identified any specific information that they assert the Danko Meredith firm impermissibly obtained that will be used to the disadvantage of the Stephens entities. In reaching this conclusion, the court has not made any explicit or implicit ruling on any issue of privilege or waiver, including the applicability or scope of Evidence Code 958 to any of the information that the Danko Meredith firm received from the bankruptcy trustee. The fact that the Danko Meredith firm impermissibly received the "client file" from the trustee is not, without more, sufficient to warrant its disqualification. (as fully reported in reporter's notes.)Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-22-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KYLE A . WITHERS OF LUBIN/OLSON, TEL. NO.1-415-981-0550 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CLAIRE Y. CHOO OF DANKO/MEREDITH, TEL. NO. 1-650-453-3600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling Defendants O&C Creditors Group, LLC and Michael Danko's motion to strike the alter ego allegations in the first amended complaint is denied. The alter ego allegations are adequately alleged. Plaintiffs have alleged sufficient ultimate facts as to both the unity of interest and inequitable result elements that are required for alter ego liability. These allegations are also sufficient for the actual controversy requirement for a declaratory relief claim since there is a current dispute whether Mr. Danko is the alter ego of O & C Creditors Group. Nor do the alter ego allegations run afoul of the rule against reverse piercing. The request for attorney's fee is stricken in three places. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =302/hk
